This is a flat note with no headers at all.

It has multiple paragraphs but no markdown heading structure. This tests the fallback chunking path where we split on paragraph boundaries instead of headers.

The content discusses baby development milestones in the first year. At three months, babies begin to hold their heads steady. At six months, many can sit with support. By nine months, most are crawling or starting to pull themselves up.

These milestones vary significantly between children and the ranges published in medical literature are wide. What matters most is the overall trajectory rather than hitting exact dates.
